reviews.review_text: As a minor player on Planet Coaster, I have not been playing very long although I've had the game awhile now I'm in my early 70's, My grandson built a new computer for me one that will play all the coaster games, I started off with Roller Coaster Tycoon years ago and got quite addicted I've progressed to Planet Coaster 2, I enjoy playing the game although I'm not very good and I persevere with it, I would highly recommend to anyone that like's platform games to think about buying it's fun and as I said very addictive

Having Played a little bit over the last 18 months or so since it launched i feel i can finally give Planet coaster 2 a review. where to start the game itself is a fantastic Builder game one of the best there is, you don't even have to build a theme park you can build pretty much anything you imagination can come up with. but coming from planet coaster 1 the change up was night an day being able to resize things an attach stuff to coasters and rides really where game changers. the addition of swimming pools and flumes was another plus. don't get me wrong the UI can be a bit of a headache at times(hopefully they'll keep trying to improve it) and at launch the game was pretty rough with very little content but now they've been putting in the work over the last 18 months to make it a really good solid game and with the promise of more DLC's to come i can only see the game getting stronger. if you want a super chill and fun building game where you can build pretty much anything and that you just get lost in for hrs on end then yea i highly recommend Planet coaster 2

I really wanted this to be good, so i waited with my review until i hopefully could give a positiv one. I can't... This game is so broken its not even funny! They break things with every update that worked before. It's really with every update. Then they release a quick hotfix for 4 or 5 of the problems a few day's later. Then its radio silent for 2-3 Months. When they have another DLC ready to press some money out of you, then they will also anounce a "fix" for the old ones. But it will only contain what is deemed necessary for Frontier. We have Bugs from the launch day's, that where never fixed. We even have bug's in DLC Ride's, that where never fixed.

The game right now has a gamebreaking bug (introduced in the last "update"). The intervall of the coaster's don't work. So if you play the Career Mode, you will be unable to complete some of the challenges. But you will hear nothing from Frontier, until they will release another DLC. Don't give Frontier any money, stay away... It's not the Frontier from the old day's anymore! Would be my 5 cents...

I really don't like how there aren't built-in strtuctures for every theme, it feels really lazy and like they expect you to either want to build everything yourself or spend ages trying to browse for something you like in the workshop.

I just want to build a big theme park, I don't want to spend hours building structures.

It's great if you want that level of detail, but personally, I don't.

If you want to spend 90% of your time tedious-building instead of managing a theme park, this is the game for you. The building system in Planet Coaster 2 is incredibly frustrating. You are stuck between two extremes: either you place these massive, bulky, premade attractions that look totally out of place in your park, or you are handed a barebones, depressing grey box for an ice cream shop that requires you to manually attach fifty microscopic pieces before it looks remotely acceptable.

The new water parks and water slides—which should have been the best part of the sequel—are clunky and awful to build. The creative freedom feels completely fake; the game constantly fights you, telling you that your custom water slide "isn't the slide the game wanted you to place," and giving you a myriad of buttons to press and making it stupidly difficult to shape and customize how you actually want.

I gave up and quit after just 3 hours because of how tedious it feels. I played Planet Coaster 1 for 17 hours and had way more fun just building coasters there. This sequel ruins the fun. Save your money.

The game is very similar to Planet Coaster 1 but has a much better story and challenge mode. It doesn't take long to unlock everything in the game but that is where the fun really takes off. There are always something fun to do or to unlock which keeps bringing you back to the game again and again.
Good game. much better and improved over the first. Path building is easy. If they fixed months-long ongoing bugs like x100 ride maintenance costs it'd be even better.

I'm sad to check "No" when asked if I would recommend this game to other players. Let me just state plainly that I'm enjoying this game. I've plugged over 400 hours into the first Planet Coaster, and I've already put 85 into this one. I deeply enjoy creating little worlds and watching these adorable little sheeple feel as if they're experiencing magic from my hard work. I'm the person who's in her happy place with a hot mug of coffee and a custom made food stall to work on. I find intense joy in placing the tiny coffee cups in cozy places. In hand making chairs to create intimate seating zones for the staff to unwind.  In imagining a resort spa and making it come to life. Yes, I could spend all day on that stuff. Am I good at it? Probably not, but I still enjoy it a lot. 

To be fair, this game is basically a rebadge of the first game. A few of the graphics are updated. Some of the sheeple look better. And there are a ton of new, fun things to add to your worlds now alongside some of the old stuff that we've come to know and love. I'm especially pleased with all the campaign maps to play through, full of new objectives. I think I've gone through all the objectives in the first Planet Coaster at least 4 times now, so I was itching for new ones. These fit the bill! Honestly, I love this game!

So why did I say I wouldn't recommend? Because it breaks my PC. Yes, my PC is on the older side now, but it does meet the specs for play. The first time it caused a problem my PC shut down as I closed the game, and the machine wouldn't turn back on again. Heartbroken, I went to bed dreading purchasing a new computer. The next morning, after some techy detective work, I finally got it to start again. Close call. The second time it broke my PC, it crashed my graphics card so hard that it corrupted the driver. Screens went black upon exiting the game. Had to wipe my graphics card driver and reinstall. I've never had any other game cause such intense problems before in my entire 30 years of gaming. This is absolutely unacceptable. I'm also sorry to say that the game itself is remarkably buggy. Rides stop recognizing scenery, sheeple get stuck in crowds walking in circles in odd places, and the camera navigation buttons don't often work on first click (w a s d) which feels frustratingly unresponsive, just to name a few. I am sad to let this game go. But it's just not worth a broken PC.

one of the most stress free games, i was caught in final exams and this game helped me get through so much. So if you playing COD or having a real ♥♥♥♥♥♥ day this game helped me and i hope it will help you.
Its a better version of pc1, but there are features missing, like theres no scenario editor.

This game looks nice, but I was not expecting to fight against the game.
It's been quite a while since the game got released and still, it is full of bugs.

Paths are doing great, until they decide to split so instead of one path, you get two different ones, for no reason. Visually it is terrible, but it also prevents the guests from passing. 
This happens on a regular basis so I need to undo then do it again and hope this time, it does not bug out. 

The ride stats seems to be heavily broken, or they might be something I am not aware of cos when I check my ride, it says fear is 8 or 9, but when doing test on the ride, the fear factor never goes upper than 5.
The game says "exceeds [enter value name]" but does not tell you what the max value is, so you need to figure out by yourself.
But in any case, my flume was terrible, some people even complained about hitting hard textures (which should actually automatically close the ride), but people were just queuing like hell.
The only thing that seems to have an impact on the rides is their age.

And last thing, I had a 100% functional flume.
Then it got stopped cause apparently, people were hitting hard floor (despite no changes have been made since ride release)
I decided to simply change the very end block and it broke my flume. Now in the middle of the flume, guest no longer have enough speed so they go backward. HOW?! WHY?!


The menu are terrible. I feel like I keep clicking and clicking again to pass from a menu to another, to "blueprint mode" to "non-blueprint mode" and again and again and again.
Does not feel smooth navigating in there, more like you keep searching for things.

And ho boy, the camera control.... FCKIN HELL

The electricity and Water management is just a pain imo.
It does not add anything interesting in game, it's just a flaw that you need to cope with. Could have been way better like maybe add batteries so we can actually use the solar panels? Get some electric management. Anything would have been better than just placing down zones to cover your electric appliances.

In the end, it's only been 13h and it feels like I mostly learned how to cope with all the bugs I faced on a regular basis and it feels bad. 
Also, the integrated workshop is pretty nice, but when I use workshop items, the game actually freezes. It also does every time I choose the circle stamp, the game works but all the UI freezes for up to 20sec.


I really hope they'll manage to fix these at some point cos apart from that, the game is actually great.
The level of control you have on your park is incredible but in it's current state and pricing (thank you Denuvo), I cannot recommend the game.

Haven't even started career mode yet -- I've spent days just building, experimenting, learning. Have loved every second of it. At first it's quite daunting, but once you get used to it all, it's incredibly fun!

This game is Frustrating beyond believe. I dont even want to get into any things that could have been improved since Planet Coaster 1 but I have been playing Career mode and had to quit the game multiple times out of frustration. Be it because of the messed up path and waiting line draw tools freaking out when trying to fill in spaces or the path and walkways just freaking out when placing entry and exit gates for a ride on a walkway forcing me to place a waiting line in the walkpath. Or when building coasters on the very limited land in career mode, the sharp locked part map edges had me rebuild entire coasters. The overall experience felt like I was playing an early access title. Watching a video of early access gameplay, i barely notice a difference.
Gamebreaking bug in the franchise mode that has existed for a year that still isn't fixed, and not really worth it for the cringey campaign and boring sandbox mode.

Planet Coaster 2 shines with creativity and gorgeous water‑park tools, but the entire experience is dragged down by its frustrating, outdated pathing system. Paths twist into unwanted curves, refuse to connect, and break the moment terrain gets involved, while guests constantly clog intersections or ignore obvious routes. Instead of enabling ambitious park designs, the path tool actively fights you, turning simple walkways into the game’s biggest obstacle and making every build feel like a battle against the system rather than a celebration of creativity. Planet coaster 1 was much better!

the frontier systems don't work i can't even sign in and on the website after linking my account, it says i have no keys to the game ridiculous 0/10 do not recommend, i cannot access the workshop or multiplayer, waste of 50 bucks
Seems, Solid. It sucks to make games these days. Players will have 100s of hours and not recommend your game.

The experience of playing this on PC is extremely frustrating and disappointing because of the following 2 main areas: Controls/Navigation & UX, Poor Campaign & Lack of Variety.

Controls & UX: The controls are clearly not well thought out for mouse & keyboard, making even navigating the camera annoying when a random palm tree gets in your way making your camera focus switch to it instead of the overall view. When building a coaster it takes you forever to build even simple turns and twists. God forbid you made a mistake on the angle of the last placed track, since if you press Ctrl+Z, then it resets to a straight track instead of the curves/angles/slope you previously had, making you have to customize it aaaall over again instead of simply tweaking the angles and slopes of the last piece placed. There is so much information, tabs, sub menus, etc. that I think they tried to include so much stuff that in the end itis really hard to find the stuff you need (And a lot of it is just useless or quite unnecessary to have) I literally had to google how to find the sub tab of the marketing campaigns that are nested inside 2 sub-menus. I could keep going but I think my point comes across.

Poor Campaign & Lack of Variety: I'm putting this together since they corelate quite a bit. I remember when I used to play other games like RCT which had a lot of diversity in their campaigns and challenges. The sceneries, objectives, sizes of parks, etc. made it interesting to play. In this campaign, which is frankly just an extremely long tutorial, it feels like you are just playing parks that are a bunch of props pasted with not much planning. They lack personality, they feel dull and pretty much all the same. The objectives are boring, monotonous and have a weird scaling in terms of difficulty. Challenges include "build 3 flat rides", "Build a pool in X area of the park" (Fun fact; they don't tell you where you can check the park area names so I had to google it to find out you had to go yet again to a sub-menu's sub-tab to see this). I think they could've gone a bit more creative, but instead feels like rushed, poorly made and without love. Oh and one last thing: the voice acting and "story" is quite a bit annoying, doesn't add to the game at all and just introduces to you some irrelevant characters with annoying personalities.

Great coaster building game for those seeking to fill that roller-coaster void. Story content was enjoyable without being too hard to progress, and the online multiplayer sandbox was still fun to sink a lot of time into. Performance is decent but struggles with really large parks (expectedly)
for lovers of coasters and water flumes it is god game
this game is amazing. but the roller coster buliding is so hard. Btw pretty good price
Amazing game, the level of complexity is amazing and I'm so glad that this game exists.
10/10 PEAK mechanics are fire and gameplay is so smooth and all the new coasters are amazing

ignore the thumbs down reviews, this game is actual PEAK.

i got this game after i upgraded my pc, and i even made a very huge park.

reviewed this in Steam Overlay btw

This game has a TON of issues. Frontier's built in workshop sucks, building is tedious, most of the tools to help you build only make things more difficult, the game does not feel good to play, the UI is atrocious, and Frontier continues to release DLC containing things that should have been in the base game.

HOWEVER

Roller coasters are ALOT smoother now, the path tool is PHENOMONAL, and the performance compared to Planet Coaster 1 is infinitely better.

For those reasons this game is just slightly worth buying, but only on sale.

In general i really like the game and some of the new tweaks with the coasters.. It has great potential, However the scenery placement like building coaster stations at least for me is some what difficult. I really wish wish the developers would at least make it like the first one that way you can knock it out and go on to build something else. Instead of having it hidden in advanced options; then a days worth of time to build it.. that is the only thing i would like to see changed outside that love the game.
everytime i play the game it says that it could not find the save folder so i cant save any of the things i make
This is i very fun and realistic game, if you love coasters, or are interested in them at all you should get this game. the game runs very smooth at ultra settings on my pc.
definitely an upgrade from the first game. it just feels a lot less clunky to build in and i really like the addition of pools and water slides.
release may have been rough, but its about everything i expected/wanted now. still missing hotels, but some of the new stuff will hold me over until they drop it for sure.
Since I was a huge fan of the Roller Coaster Tycoon series, this game is the perfect successor! I love this game and I will spend many more hours into it.
This game can allow people to find their creativity in building coasters. Also, Cheese...

This game is the BEST, and NO, i am NOT joking
This game was worth the 22 dollars and 49 cents
You are able to make Theme Parks with Unlimited Money through Sandbox Mode
you can make custom rides and add in Pre-Made Rides
you can actually ride the rides by clicking on the ride, and click the camera button when the ride starts,
Please Download this Game

One of the best creative games. The building features are some of the best of any creative building game. I find myself being to build whatever my mind can craft up. Lastly, the development team listens the community making feature we desire.

peak. just peak smoother cameras fantastic realism
also get it now while it is on discount

Pros: 
        This game is awsome and objectively better than the first. The New menu is confusing at first (I'm still trying to figure out some of it.) But it is so rewarding, With the new menu And new features they added to this game you can truly make anything you want. The flumes and arcades add more stuff to do (which I thought the first game lacked content becuase all you could build is coasters and rides you can't edit.)

Cons:

The game is quite laggy even on a standard nice pc. The lag got so annoying at a point that you have to play game in Medium graphics quality to even be considered playable and even then it still lags! You still can't edit the rides (besides the flumes and coasters,) yes I know you can add scenery but i mean more like being able to change the height of a drop tower and stuff like that. The
very good goood game for real coasters. tips for begeners use baking offens for a deffernt coaster so for inverted - .

quiet good mabey just for exprensted player at disgning
but amazing
overall good game, i think if you like rollercoasters and theme park, or have a lot of creativity, you would enjoy this game
it is my creative outlet. story mode can get frustrating, but once you learn the controls - you're unstoppable. loveeeeee this game.
Built a couple of coasters and then realized they would turn my guests into soup. 10/10 game.
After 16 hours in I made a negative review. Meanwhile I changed my mind. The game still has it's problems and bugs but there's so much to love about it that now I recommend it.
Such a beautiful game. It even got dramatically better since release. Now really is the best time to get into it and build what ever you can imagine.
This game is the best game to do anything you ever wanted. I made a bus that runs people over.
it would be such a blessing if the game would be compatible with macs instead of windows only

Great successor. Missing things like hotels from pc1 and long wanted features like moving train stations/conveyor belt stations and some coaster models (please add the mack stryker i beg you), but other than that everything you could wish for in a Theme Park Sim/ Coaster construction sim.

I was a bit skeptical of it being an improvement over 1, but after buying it I found it to be a great improvement. The path building is considerably better than 1, and the pools are fun too. I think it feels like less content overall if you include the dlcs from 1, but overall the quality of life upgrades make this one worth it.
most realistic and fun roller-coaster game and way better then the first game
Best theme park game on the market! It might not be the best COASTER builder game but it is 100% fun to play!!!!!!
Amazing game. Anything is possible to create. I love creating my own personal ideal gothic wonderland full of thrill rides and stunning scenery. Absolutely love this game. 10/10

A great turnaround since it launched. Great graphics, really great scenery builder. A work of love and art, great 3D models, great artstyle, good music. Good to see that there is support to make it better and the devs are listening to the community. This games needs more love. The bad: Management is lacking, it feels like chores instead of actually fixing economy by deciding what to build next to save your park and get more customers. It's not a management game and i want the tycoon factor to be equally as important as the building. I want my decisions to matter and have a robuust economy, and here it is lacking. If the management and the user interface could get a revision and an upgrade, it would be my dream game. But right now, i feel a bit underwhelmed by it. The building and coaster design and dark rides are by far the best i've ever seen. The management is lacking and the interface is a huge let down. I hope the game gets more time to get where it needs to be. It deserves it so hard. Recommend tho. It's the best themepark builder out there, but not a tycoon game.

I've had the game in my library since launch, due to the abyssmal launch I hadn't really played it. I'm finally now starting to play it.

There's a lot more on offer here then in Planco 1, but also a lot less. In terms of scenery, the scaling tools make the current scenery selection quite flexible. The ride roster is pretty OK (apart from dark ride options). What the game lacks is DLC like we had with Planco 1. I'm afraid Frontier has given up on this game (1 DLC every 6 months). If what's currently here interests you then defintely go for it. If there're things you're missing in terms of rides and scenery, don't hold your breath.

I'd love for Frontier to prove me wrong though.

The graphics and realistic game play is awesome for running a theme park. You can immerse yourself in this game as far as your imagination can carry you and more. The dislike for me is the arrow keys do not operate when I am in building mode and constructing a building.  They work fine when placing items in my park but when in the building mode or building scenery for coaster station it is tricky.

Was fun just like the first game. Pools and flumes were a good addition. There a quite a few bugs that need fixing, but nothing game breaking. I don't like have much they lock behind DLC. Like the first, the community carries this game. The community workshop is amazing.

This game is a blast... if you play it right! This game is more about slowing down to enjoy things, not blowing through the level objectives ASAP. Most objectives are fairly easy, so it allows the player to take time decorating and making the park look nice. If that is the pace you like, then I absolutely recommend the game.

this game is no challenge its made for kids or console,the first game was a challenge you had to train staff etc and your restaurants took time to add music change food etc ,,,,this game is not as good as the first game there is no depth what so ever

Genuinely frustrating game. You can't train employees, rides break down all the time and you can't schedule maintenance, research carries over levels but means it's now way too easy to get (you assign one employee research duty and you can get 50,000 in one day - where's the challenge?), the additions of power and water filtration are nice on paper but an absolute pain to deal with, scenery is much more annoying than in the first game.

Basically everything they added resolved problems no one actually thought were problems while creating a whole ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of new problems that were completely unnecessary. 

Why does the bathroom start to stink every 5 minutes? Do all the guests have Crohn's Disease?

Also the dialogue is just about the worst I've ever seen in a video game.

Trash. 3/10.

The game run okay-ish after the couple of updates frontier released. But all in all it is still behind its possibilites. Best would have been to either overhaul PC1 and sell it as an upgrade or to release it 12 months later. Currently it feels really bad not that long after release where PC1 began to shine. It looks like frontier abandoned most of the plans for the games. Besides Sandbox there is no real challenge and a lot of content from PC1 still missing. 
If you are big fan to the franchise, go for it. If you like economy simulation, skip it.

There is a lot to like about this game. If you want creative freedom, this may be your jam. And I am having fun with it.

But, in career mode, I was tasked with creating a flume. I was having trouble with the controls and ended up making a flume that did not end with people going into the pool, but on the pavement, It looked like road rash would be the best outcome. And in sandbox mode, I am fine with that, if you want to unleash your inner Jigsaw or whatever. But the fact that the park owner pops up and congratulates me on a job well done, and it meets the scenario's requirements, that is just broken. It seems like the test rides that tell you G-forces and nausea ratings are just window dressing, and maybe people won't ride based on that - which again, in sandbox mode, fine, but this should not be OK in career mode.
It was cool but I wish the game had more tracks and more special tracks like alex ojoda on youtube

What have they done with the pathing tool it's a mess. It was at least usable in planet zoo, but this is terrible. Pools. Who thought this was a good idea? This the new core feature to this game and it's so one note. I just don't get why they put it in as a selling feature. This should have been a tool in addition to like a resort building feature to the game. Could you imagine building a theme park and a resort? That would be cool. Pools alone are not a game selling addition to a game about building theme parks. Loved the original planet coaster. Had so much fun with it, this simply isn't worth it. Play the original.
I hated the UI in Planet Coaster. I foolishly thought it would be better in Planet Coaster 2.  It's not.  For me it makes the game unusable.

I can't even get 10 hours into the game without encountering game breaking bugs. These are not minor bugs, these are Kwalasha-sized bugs. I thought by waiting a year or two to get this game it would be fixed and as good as the first game. Guess the joke's on me! I've seen nothing aside from the path tool and the pools that is better than in PC1. And there's issues with those, too!

Its my fault for not doing any research, I assumed that there would be improvements from the first one and its horrible path system and general construction nightmare. Its still needlessly tedious to try to have any kind of symmetry or add design elements at all without rage quitting and just accepting that its going to look terrible. I don't want to spend 2 hours trying to make a pool not have jagged edged and a bumpy bottom- which I am not convinced is even possible.  Its extremely unintuitive and clunky, requiring you to exit out and go back in over and over rather than just selecting what you actually want. You shouldn't have to google complicated work arounds in order that don't work. I do not understand how it is so bad, so many games have this system figured out, and I would have assumed that the new game would address those complaints. Its actually worse in some ways. The game sends alerts but offers no solutions on how to fix those alerts, and searching online you see that its been a problem for years with no answers and no fixes. I would accept these things in early access but for a full released game its unacceptable. I have already sunk 17 hours into giving this a chance, I would not recommend unless you are fine with ripping your hair out from frustration and saying wtf every 10 minutes.
Very good graphics. Only down side is that if you have many trees, it is very hard to place rocks, building, and etc.
Rough start but they have slowly moulded this into a solid buy. It's well worth the upgrade from planco1, yet to try the toy add on, still plenty for me to crack on with for now.

This kept crashing as soon as the startup screen ended
I dont think my specs are too outdated but i tried everything 

Ive seen this game on youtube and i think its really cool so im gonna give a good review anyway
the best roller coaster sim out there. WAY better than the OG and worth your money.

First impressions aren't great. The career mode is buggy and barely works, with the game not recognising that you have completed objectives. I've tried the same process in different saves and it only counts as a success half the time.

The voice acting of the guides is annoying - thank goodness they're skippable. It's a very strange addition.

The power stations idea is cool at first but becomes a chore, like I said is also a buggy mess. The new rides aren't that interesting and I just feel that this could have been DLC for Planet Coaster 1; which in of itself is riddled with DLC alone I suppose.

You still can't make guests which would be a great feature, despite this being suggested by many of us players. I'd love to create me, my friends and family to watch them wander around the parks. You can make your avatar with extremely limited options for customisation.

Water parks/pools/slides were a very requested addition from the first Planet Coaster but they are fun for about 5 minutes then become a horrid chore to maintain. Just not very fun.

I LOVED Planet Coaster 1. But now I am starting to regret my purchase for this sequel. I hope I end up enjoying the game more as time passes.
Great sequel to the original. Pathing is a greatly improved process. Scenery is great. Runs like a dream with all setting as high as they'll go on this machine.
Pools are great and the new path-building is awsome. however we could use some more scenary and pre build items.

I've played a ton of the first Planet Coaster, and this one is very similar and the new features are great. I love that you can set the daily schedule for your workers, however it's really frustrating to navigate the menus to assign hourly tasks. When you set the filter to see specific staff and zones, after you schedule a workers timeslot, the filters reset. You have to redo the filters each time for each hour. The same thing happens in other menus, and it's very frustrating and makes some of the new features hard to use.
Finally i can build my own liminal pool rooms and torture guests

I put over 500hrs into Planet Coaster 1, and I would imagine I'm gonna spend just as much time in this sequel. It's not much different graphically than PC1 - but that's a good thing, since (for example) I couldn't make the jump to Cities Skylines 2 due to absurd performance issues despite my pretty decent rig. Mercifully, PC2 runs at a buttery smooth 60fps for the most part. 
I'm currently working my way through the career mode in order to learn all the little nuances and new features and it's great fun so far.  One I've completed that I will create my own parks.

I have no problem recommending this game to you. It's like Planet Coaster 1, but with so many improvements - it is worth the price, and you will often find it at a 55% discount (which is when I bought it).
I like steel (and sometimes wooden, and sometimes both) machines that have airtime. Also I made a coaster that pulls 236 G'S. Try to beat that

Way more complicated than it needs to be. It's a game. There are plenty of things to do, but too many. If you are looking to get a job, this may be a good start. I adore the first version, & have played all of the previous RCT's when they came out. This one, just isn't as fun I'm sorry to say.
You can do a bunch, the camera angles are fine, but I'm gonna uninstall, go back & play the other options I have. I was hoping for a little more thrill & fun. Oh well.

If you enjoy theme parks and have always wanted to create your own with great range of coasters, flat rides and track rides. Your creations are limited by your imagination. From dropping pre made blue prints, downloading others creations, to creating you own and sharing with the community.

Please improve the pathing tools. It just feels incredibly clunky especially when trying to connect two paths on slightly uneven terrain, one being drawn around a pool and the other being made the line tool. It keeps trying to make them in to two separate paths. I spent an hour trying to get it right but just gave up in the end. I also don't like the restriction on deleting or moving nature items in the Career mode, IE In the swim of things, Beachfront 2 area, on the sand there is some trees and rock that cannot be moved or deleted. I wanted to use that space for a large pool there to then have a big flume ride down to it taking advantage of the near by hill, but had to settle on a small pool. It's these sort of things make me feel frustrated and feel like I'm fighting the game's tools to make/manage a park that I want. Maybe it a skill issue. Maybe I should just skip the career mode for now stick with sandbox mode instead like I did with the previous title. I probably would want a refund if didn't get it on sale.

[h3]Longing to play, waiting to drown.[/h3]
I came to Planet Coaster 2 as a fan of the first game; bought it the second it became available for pre-order, and have been patiently checking in with every update for over a year, praying that they will fix the one thing that's holding me back: [b]The Interface.[/b]

[h3]Oh, God: The User Interface…[/h3]
Coming over from Planet Coaster 1, I won't pretend that the UI in PC1 was flawless, but it was [b]functional[/b], [b]responsive[/b], and [b]user friendly[/b]; it never felt impracticle to learn the workflow for different tools and menus.
By comparison, Planet Coaster 2's UI is [b]convoluted[/b], [b]sluggish[/b], and [b]frustrating[/b] to work with; there are menus within menus with submenus often found in strange places; there is a quarter-second delay on every tooltip popup; there's a bizzare amount of added negative space in the various asset creation menus [i](every asset listed in the scenery menu has a ten pixel gap between every icon and, if your cursor is over this gap, the tooltip will not display)[/i]… I persevered with it for fifteen hours, and never came away feeling like I was getting closer to understanding it.

[h3]Want to love, can't.[/h3]
I want to play this game — I really do! The visuals are wonderful; the soundtrack is great; the potential to make something [i]is[/i] there, but I can't get past the current interface — especially when I've played PC1 and none of these problems ever existed. As it stands: The current user experience with this UI design is just awkward. It's been eighteen months and I'm still waiting to see if the menu will ever be fixed.
